message.content:
Adversarially review the Batch-1 diff on branch fix/race-batch1-evidence-substrate in /home/polaris/wt/faithoff (levers B, E, F + re-anchor). Run: git -C /home/polaris/wt/faithoff diff --stat and read the changed hunks. FOCUS DIMENSION: BYTE-IDENTITY-OFF: for EACH new flag, confirm default-off is truly byte-identical to pre-batch behavior (this is the release invariant).
Report concrete issues as file:line with a one-line failure scenario each; if clean on your dimension say CLEAN. Do NOT fix — just report. Do NOT read CLAUDE.md.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll start by examining the diff on the branch.

6	     # cleanly. Digit-anchored on both sides, so code/JSON 'a[3].[i]' is untouched.
7	     # Default '0' = leave the '].[' glue as-is = today's byte-identical render.
8	     'PG_CITATION_INLINE_GLUE_COLLAPSE': '0',
9	+    # LEVER B (citation re-anchoring): when truthy, a claim sentence whose cited
10	+    # [#ev] token points at a SECONDARY source is UPSTREAM re-pointed to a PRIMARY
11	+    # row (same normalized finding/numbers, higher tier, journal/DOI) BEFORE
12	+    # strict_verify runs — a grounding-SOURCE selection, never a sentence drop/
13	+    # filter. Default '' = off => the [#ev] tokens are byte-identical.
14	+    'PG_CITATION_REANCHOR_PRIMARY': '',
15	+    # LEVER F (canonicalize works): when truthy, the GLOBAL bibliography unit
16	+    # becomes the CANONICAL WORK instead of the raw evidence_id. Upstream rows
17	+    # already carry same_work grouping (55 same_work_groups on task-72), but the
18	+    # global bibliography (_merge_bibliographies) dedups only by evidence_id, so
19	+    # ONE work fetched from several mirror URLs / manifestations appears as
20	+    # [5][6][7]. When on, every member evidence_id of a work (identified by the
21	+    # SHARED deterministic same-work key: normalized source URL, else DOI, else
22	+    # folded title + discriminator — parsed generically from the row's OWN
23	+    # locators, NO task literals) folds to a SINGLE canonical [N], preferring the
24	+    # DOI/primary manifestation for the surviving entry. The remap runs through
25	+    # the existing _remap_section_markers_to_global path and collapses resulting
26	+    # adjacent duplicate markers ('[5][5]' -> '[5]'). Pure deterministic
27	+    # identity + numbering — NO model, NO network, NO sentence drop/filter (a
28	+    # remapped-away entry's evidence_id still resolves, it just shares a number).
29	+    # Default '' = off => one bibliography entry per evidence_id as today =>
30	+    # byte-identical bibliography + inline markers.
31	+    'PG_CANONICAL_WORK_BIBLIOGRAPHY': '',
32	     'PG_CITEFIX_ENABLED': '0',
33	     'PG_CITEFIX_KEYWORD_MIN': '3',
34	     'PG_CITEFIX_SEMANTIC_THRESHOLD': '0.50',
35	@@ -349,6 +372,33 @@ CONFIG_DEFAULTS: dict[str, str | None] = {
36	     'PG_FETCH_MAX_RETRIES': '3',
37	     'PG_FETCH_MIN_BODY_CHARS': '0',
38	     'PG_FETCH_NONEMPTY_RAW_FLOOR': '200',
39	+    # LEVER E (fetch-until-usable): when truthy, a fetched row that comes back a
40	+    # STUB (extracted body below the salvage floor) AND carries a DOI or PMID is
41	+    # routed through the shared deterministic metadata-API salvage lane
42	+    # (frame_fetcher CrossRef -> OpenAlex -> PubMed -> Semantic Scholar richest
43	+    # abstract, an OA-URL scrape via the existing OA resolver) to recover full
44	+    # text or a sufficiently-informative abstract — a CITABLE span — BEFORE the
45	+    # row is labeled `stub`. On a salvage hit the body is upgraded to ok=True; on
46	+    # a miss the row keeps its `stub` verdict (retained for disclosure, not
47	+    # citation — §-1.3 label-not-drop). This RUNS ONLY as an upstream fetch-layer
48	+    # recovery — it adds NO post-generation entailment / verification / filtering
49	+    # gate, and reuses the existing frame_fetcher clients (no new LLM call).
50	+    # Default '' = OFF => the salvage lane is never entered => byte-identical to
51	+    # today (the min-body gate itself stays off because PG_FETCH_MIN_BODY_CHARS
52	+    # is unchanged at '0'). Read at CALL time (env-overridable per run).
53	+    'PG_FETCH_STUB_SALVAGE': '',
54	+    # LEVER E: the effective min-body floor (chars) that DEFINES a "stub" for the
55	+    # salvage lane ABOVE. A real journal body is tens of thousands of chars; a
56	+    # paywall / abstract shell is a few hundred, so ~800 chars cleanly separates a
57	+    # citable span from a dead shell. This floor is consulted ONLY when
58	+    # PG_FETCH_STUB_SALVAGE is truthy — it does NOT touch PG_FETCH_MIN_BODY_CHARS
59	+    # (which stays '0'/off), so default-off remains byte-identical. Overridable.
60	+    'PG_FETCH_STUB_SALVAGE_MIN_BODY_CHARS': '800',
61	+    # LEVER E: bounded number of distinct deterministic metadata sources the
62	+    # salvage lane will consult per stub row (CrossRef, OpenAlex, PubMed, S2 = 4).
63	+    # A CAP, never a target — keeps the recovery bounded per LAW-VI. Consulted
64	+    # only when PG_FETCH_STUB_SALVAGE is truthy.
65	+    'PG_FETCH_STUB_SALVAGE_MAX_SOURCES': '4',
66	     'PG_FETCH_RETRY_BASE': '2.0',
67	     'PG_FETCH_RETRY_MAX': '30.0',
68	     'PG_FETCH_SHELL_VOCAB_GATE': '1',
69	@@ -722,6 +772,17 @@ CONFIG_DEFAULTS: dict[str, str | None] = {
70	     'PG_RISK_QUORUM_MIN': '2',
71	     'PG_ROLE_ALLOW_FALLBACKS': '',
72	     'PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_RETRIES': '2',
73	+    # LEVER B (source eligibility): when truthy, the RQ's OWN stated constraints
74	+    # (source_types / languages / recency — parsed generically from the prompt and
75	+    # cached in protocol['_rq_constraints']) DEMOTE ineligible citable rows at the
76	+    # generation-pool boundary (weight in (0,1), tail-partitioned, KEPT + disclosed
77	+    # — WEIGHT-not-DROP). Default '' = off => the eligibility map is empty =>
78	+    # selection sort key + tail partition are byte-identical.
79	+    'PG_RQ_SOURCE_ELIGIBILITY_ENFORCE': '',

81	+    # (mirrors PG_SCOPE_DEMOTE_WEIGHT / the date-window demote). Clamped to (0,1);
82	+    # empty => the module default. Only consulted when the enforce flag is on.

84	     'PG_RUN_DIFF_TIER_PP': None,

86	     'PG_S2_KEYWORD_MAX_TERMS': '8',
87	diff --git a/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py b/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py
88	index 684a60e..b4e1208 100644
89	--- a/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py
90	+++ b/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py
91	@@ -8059,6 +8059,135 @@ async def _call_limitations(
92	 # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
93	 
94	 
95	+# LEVER F (canonicalize works): when ``PG_CANONICAL_WORK_BIBLIOGRAPHY`` is truthy the GLOBAL
96	+# bibliography unit becomes the CANONICAL WORK (identified by the SHARED deterministic same-work
97	+# key) instead of the raw evidence_id — so ONE work fetched from several mirror URLs /
98	+# manifestations folds to a SINGLE ``[N]`` (a paper cited as ``[5][6][7]`` becomes ``[5]``),
99	+# with every member evidence_id remapping to that canonical number and the resulting adjacent
100	+# duplicate markers collapsed. Default OFF => one entry per evidence_id => byte-identical.
101	+_ENV_CANONICAL_WORK_BIBLIOGRAPHY = "PG_CANONICAL_WORK_BIBLIOGRAPHY"
102	+
103	+
104	+def _canonical_work_bibliography_enabled() -> bool:
105	+    """LEVER F kill switch (``PG_CANONICAL_WORK_BIBLIOGRAPHY``). Default OFF => the global
106	+    bibliography carries one entry per evidence_id (today's behavior) => byte-identical output."""
107	+    return resolve(_ENV_CANONICAL_WORK_BIBLIOGRAPHY).strip().lower() in (
108	+        "1", "true", "yes", "on",
109	+    )
110	+
111	+
112	+def _biblio_work_key(entry: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
113	+    """The SHARED same-work identity of a bibliography entry (LEVER F).
114	+
115	+    Reuses ``finding_dedup._same_work_key`` — the SINGLE deterministic identity contract
116	+    (normalized source URL first, else normalized DOI, else folded title + discriminator) that
117	+    the upstream same-work consolidators already key on — so the bibliography folds EXACTLY the
118	+    works upstream grouped. Reads the entry's OWN locators generically (no task literals). A
119	+    blank key (no usable URL/DOI/title+discriminator) means "own singleton work": we fall back
120	+    to the evidence_id so an un-keyable entry is NEVER merged into another work.
121	+
122	+    The biblio entry stores its URL under ``url`` (``_num_for`` in provenance_generator); the
123	+    shared key reads ``source_url`` OR ``url``, so passing the entry directly is correct.
124	+    """
125	+    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.finding_dedup import _same_work_key  # noqa: PLC0415
126	+
127	+    key = _same_work_key(entry)
128	+    if key:
129	+        return key
130	+    return "ev:" + str(entry.get("evidence_id", "") or id(entry))
131	+
132	+
133	+def _entry_locator_rank(entry: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[int, int, int]:
134	+    """Preference rank for choosing the CANONICAL manifestation within a same-work group
135	+    (LEVER F). LOWER sorts first => becomes the surviving canonical entry.
136	+
137	+    Prefers, in order: a DOI-bearing (primary/citable) manifestation, then a PMID-bearing one,
138	+    then a URL-bearing one — the primary/DOI/English manifestation for claim support. Pure
139	+    metadata-presence tie-break; no model, no network.
140	+    """
141	+    has_doi = 1 if str(entry.get("doi", "") or "").strip() else 0
142	+    has_pmid = 1 if str(entry.get("pmid", "") or "").strip() else 0
143	+    has_url = 1 if str(entry.get("url", "") or "").strip() else 0
144	+    # Negate presence flags so a present locator sorts BEFORE an absent one under ascending sort.
145	+    return (-has_doi, -has_pmid, -has_url)
146	+
147	+
148	+def _canonicalize_work_bibliography(
149	+    biblio: list[dict[str, Any]],
150	+) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], dict[str, int]]:
151	+    """Collapse a per-evidence_id bibliography into a per-WORK bibliography (LEVER F).
152	+
153	+    Groups entries by their SHARED same-work key, elects ONE canonical entry per group
154	+    (``_entry_locator_rank``: DOI > PMID > URL manifestation), renumbers the canonical entries
155	+    contiguously in FIRST-APPEARANCE order (stable — the first evidence_id of each work keeps
156	+    the low number the reader already saw), and returns:
157	+      * the canonical bibliography (one entry per work, ``num`` reassigned), and
158	+      * an ``evidence_id -> canonical_num`` map covering EVERY member evidence_id (the elected
159	+        canonical AND its folded mirrors), so the inline-marker remap can point every member's
160	+        ``[N]`` at the one canonical number.
161	+
162	+    Keep-all: no entry is DROPPED from the world — a folded mirror's evidence_id still resolves
163	+    to its work's number; it simply shares the canonical ``[N]`` instead of getting its own.
164	+    The canonical entry additionally carries ``same_work_member_evidence_ids`` (all member ids)
165	+    for any downstream breadth/coverage consumer, WITHOUT changing which [N] renders.
166	+    """
167	+    # Preserve first-appearance order of works; collect members per work.
168	+    work_order: list[str] = []
169	+    members_by_work: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]] = {}
170	+    for entry in biblio:
171	+        wkey = _biblio_work_key(entry)
172	+        if wkey not in members_by_work:
173	+            members_by_work[wkey] = []
174	+            work_order.append(wkey)
175	+        members_by_work[wkey].append(entry)
176	+
177	+    canonical_biblio: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
178	+    ev_to_canonical_num: dict[str, int] = {}
179	+    for new_num, wkey in enumerate(work_order, 1):
180	+        members = members_by_work[wkey]
181	+        # Elect the canonical manifestation: best locator rank, ties broken by the entry's
182	+        # ORIGINAL number so the choice is deterministic and prefers the earlier-seen row.
183	+        canonical_entry = min(
184	+            members,
185	+            key=lambda e: (_entry_locator_rank(e), int(e.get("num", 0) or 0)),
186	+        )
187	+        new_entry = dict(canonical_entry)
188	+        new_entry["num"] = new_num
189	+        member_ev_ids = [
190	+            str(m.get("evidence_id", "") or "") for m in members
191	+            if str(m.get("evidence_id", "") or "")
192	+        ]
193	+        new_entry["same_work_member_evidence_ids"] = member_ev_ids
194	+        canonical_biblio.append(new_entry)
195	+        # Map EVERY member evidence_id (canonical + mirrors) to this one canonical number.
196	+        for m in members:
197	+            m_ev = str(m.get("evidence_id", "") or "")
198	+            if m_ev:
199	+                ev_to_canonical_num[m_ev] = new_num
200	+    return canonical_biblio, ev_to_canonical_num
201	+
202	+
203	+# LEVER F: two adjacent numeric citation markers that (after canonical remap) resolve to the SAME
204	+# number are a mirror double-cite of ONE work — collapse '[5][5]' -> '[5]'. Only fires on the
205	+# canonicalized path; the legacy per-evidence_id path never produces adjacent identical markers.
206	+_ADJACENT_DUP_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\[(\d+)\](?=\[\1\])")
207	+
208	+
209	+def _collapse_adjacent_duplicate_markers(text: str) -> str:
210	+    """Collapse runs of the SAME numeric citation marker to a single marker (LEVER F).
211	+
212	+    ``[5][5][5]`` -> ``[5]``. Only IDENTICAL adjacent numbers are collapsed (a genuine
213	+    ``[5][6]`` multi-cite is untouched), so no distinct citation is ever lost.
214	+    """
215	+    prev = None
216	+    out = text
217	+    # Iterate to a fixed point so runs of 3+ identical markers fully collapse.
218	+    while out != prev:
219	+        prev = out
220	+        out = _ADJACENT_DUP_MARKER_RE.sub("", out)
221	+    return out
222	+
223	+
224	 def _merge_bibliographies(
225	     section_slices: list[list[dict[str, Any]]],
226	 ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
227	@@ -8089,6 +8218,12 @@ def _merge_bibliographies(
228	         new_entry = dict(entry)
229	         new_entry["num"] = i
230	         final.append(new_entry)
231	+    # LEVER F (canonicalize works): OFF => return the per-evidence_id bibliography above
232	+    # (byte-identical). ON => fold same-work mirror entries into ONE canonical entry per work
233	+    # so a paper fetched from several URLs / manifestations renders as a single [N] (the remap
234	+    # in _remap_section_markers_to_global points every member ev_id at that canonical number).
235	+    if _canonical_work_bibliography_enabled():
236	+        final, _ = _canonicalize_work_bibliography(final)
237	     return final
238	 
239	 
240	@@ -8103,8 +8238,26 @@ def _remap_section_markers_to_global(
241	     I-deepfix-002 (#1363): this NEVER drops a marker. Every [N] in already
242	     strict_verify-PASSED section prose maps to its global number; off-topic
243	     cite-suppression is handled upstream at the enrichment selection only, so a
244	-    verified section citation is never orphaned here."""
245	-    ev_to_global = {b["evidence_id"]: b["num"] for b in global_biblio}
246	+    verified section citation is never orphaned here.
247	+
248	+    LEVER F (canonicalize works): when the canonical-work bibliography is on, a canonical
249	+    entry carries ``same_work_member_evidence_ids`` — the evidence_ids of every mirror
250	+    manifestation that folded into it. We map EACH of those member ev_ids to the canonical
251	+    entry's number, so a section that cited two mirrors of ONE work points both markers at the
252	+    single canonical [N]; the resulting adjacent duplicate markers ('[5][5]') are then collapsed.
253	+    OFF => the canonical members list is absent, ``ev_to_global`` is the plain per-evidence_id
254	+    map, and no collapse runs => byte-identical."""
255	+    _canonical_on = _canonical_work_bibliography_enabled()
256	+    ev_to_global: dict[str, int] = {}
257	+    for b in global_biblio:
258	+        num = b["num"]
259	+        ev_to_global[b["evidence_id"]] = num
260	+        if _canonical_on:
261	+            # Fold every member ev_id of this work onto its canonical number, so a marker that
262	+            # points at a mirror manifestation still resolves to the single canonical [N].
263	+            for member_ev in (b.get("same_work_member_evidence_ids") or []):
264	+                if member_ev:
265	+                    ev_to_global[member_ev] = num
266	     remapped: list[str] = []
267	     for sect in section_results:
268	         if not sect.verified_text:
269	@@ -8128,6 +8281,10 @@ def _remap_section_markers_to_global(
270	             return f"[{g}]" if g else match.group(0)
271	 
272	         text = re.sub(r"\[(\d+)\]", _replace, text)
273	+        # LEVER F: two mirror manifestations of ONE work now share a number, so the prose may
274	+        # carry '[5][5]' — collapse identical adjacent markers to a single [5]. OFF => no-op.
275	+        if _canonical_on:
276	+            text = _collapse_adjacent_duplicate_markers(text)
277	         remapped.append(text)
278	     return remapped
279	 
280	diff --git a/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/evidence_selector.py b/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/evidence_selector.py
281	index 6da9230..bdc0f54 100644
282	--- a/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/evidence_selector.py
283	+++ b/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/evidence_selector.py
284	@@ -2234,6 +2234,7 @@ def _relevance_floor_selection(
285	     url_to_content_relevance_weight: dict[str, float] | None = None,
286	     url_to_date_weight: dict[str, float] | None = None,
287	     url_to_scope_weight: dict[str, float] | None = None,
288	+    url_to_eligibility_weight: dict[str, float] | None = None,

433	index 21854f7..a1b41f7 100644

437	 # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
438	 # Layer 4 — Orchestrator
439	 # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
440	+def salvage_stub_span(
441	+    *,
442	+    doi: str = "",
443	+    pmid: str = "",
444	+    client: httpx.Client | None = None,
445	+    max_sources: int = 4,
446	+) -> tuple[str, str]:
447	+    """LEVER E (fetch-until-usable) — SHARED deterministic salvage lane.
448	+
449	+    GENERALIZED extraction of the DOI/PMID abstract-recovery chain that already
450	+    powers ``_fetch_frame_entity_inner`` for V30 contract entities, exposed as a
451	+    standalone helper so ANY stub fetch row that carries a DOI or PMID (not just
452	+    a compiled ``EvidenceBinding``) can recover a CITABLE span — full text or a
453	+    sufficiently-informative abstract — from the deterministic metadata APIs.
454	+
455	+    Consults, in bounded priority order and REUSING the module's existing
456	+    clients/parsers (no new HTTP surface, no LLM call):
457	+
458	+      1. CrossRef  ``/works/{doi}``           — abstract (+ title anchor)
459	+      2. OpenAlex  ``/works/{doi}``           — inverted-index abstract
460	+      3. PubMed    EFetch ``{pmid}``          — deterministic abstract XML
461	+      4. Semantic Scholar ``/paper/DOI:{doi}`` — abstract
462	+
463	+    then returns the RICHEST abstract via ``_pick_richest_abstract`` (the SAME
464	+    deterministic longest-wins selection the frame path uses). At most
465	+    ``max_sources`` DOI-keyed sources are consulted (a CAP, LAW-VI bounded).
466	+
467	+    Unlike the live_retriever OA resolver (Unpaywall-URL scrape + PubMed, DOI
468	+    REQUIRED), this lane:
469	+      * works for a PMID-ONLY row (no DOI) via the PubMed abstract, and
470	+      * consults CrossRef / OpenAlex / S2, which the OA resolver never does.
471	+
472	+    Returns ``(span, source_label)`` where ``source_label`` is one of the
473	+    ``_pick_richest_abstract`` labels (``crossref_abstract`` / ``openalex_abstract``
474	+    / ``pubmed_abstract`` / ``s2_abstract``), or ``("", "none")`` when every
475	+    source misses. Fail-SAFE by contract: every internal error is swallowed and
476	+    yields ``("", "none")`` so the caller falls through to its existing stub
477	+    behavior. Deterministic given upstream API state — no randomness, no
478	+    wall-clock in the returned span.
479	+
480	+    Recovery only ADDS content (§-1.3: never drops a row). This is an UPSTREAM
481	+    fetch-layer helper — it contains NO entailment / verification / sentence-
482	+    dropping / filtering gate.
483	+    """
484	+    doi = (doi or "").strip()
485	+    pmid = (pmid or "").strip()
486	+    if not doi and not pmid:
487	+        return "", "none"
488	+
489	+    owns_client = client is None
490	+    if client is None:
491	+        client = httpx.Client(timeout=_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT)
492	+    try:
493	+        abstract_crossref: str | None = None
494	+        abstract_openalex: str | None = None
495	+        abstract_pubmed: str | None = None
496	+        abstract_s2: str | None = None
497	+        cr_title: str | None = None
498	+        sources_used = 0
499	+        cap = max(0, int(max_sources))
500	+
501	+        # 1. CrossRef abstract (DOI-keyed).
502	+        if doi and sources_used < cap:
503	+            try:
504	+                cr_data, _a, _t = _call_crossref(client, doi)
505	+                sources_used += 1
506	+                if cr_data is not None:
507	+                    parsed = _parse_crossref_response(cr_data)
508	+                    abstract_crossref = parsed.get("abstract")
509	+                    cr_title = parsed.get("title")
510	+            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-SAFE, never break fetch
511	+                pass
512	+
513	+        # 2. OpenAlex inverted-index abstract (DOI-keyed).
514	+        if doi and sources_used < cap:
515	+            try:
516	+                oa_data, _a, _t = _call_openalex(client, doi)
517	+                sources_used += 1
518	+                if oa_data is not None:
519	+                    abstract_openalex = _parse_openalex_response(oa_data).get(
520	+                        "abstract"
521	+                    )
522	+            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
523	+                pass
524	+
525	+        # 3. PubMed EFetch abstract (PMID-keyed — the PMID-only recovery path).
526	+        if pmid and sources_used < cap:
527	+            try:
528	+                pm_body, _a, _t = _call_pubmed(client, pmid)
529	+                sources_used += 1
530	+                if pm_body is not None:
531	+                    abstract_pubmed = _parse_pubmed_xml(pm_body).get("abstract")
532	+            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
533	+                pass
534	+
535	+        # 4. Semantic Scholar abstract (DOI-keyed).
536	+        if doi and sources_used < cap:
537	+            try:
538	+                s2_data, _a, _t = _call_s2(client, doi)
539	+                sources_used += 1
540	+                if s2_data is not None:
541	+                    abstract_s2 = _parse_s2_response(s2_data).get("abstract")
542	+            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
543	+                pass
544	+
545	+        span, source_label = _pick_richest_abstract(
546	+            crossref=abstract_crossref,
547	+            openalex=abstract_openalex,
548	+            pubmed=abstract_pubmed,
549	+            s2=abstract_s2,
550	+        )
551	+        if span:
552	+            logger.info(
553	+                "[frame_fetcher] salvage_stub_span HIT doi=%s pmid=%s "
554	+                "source=%s chars=%d — recovered a citable span from the "
555	+                "deterministic metadata APIs (title=%r)",
556	+                doi or "-", pmid or "-", source_label, len(span),
557	+                (cr_title or "")[:60],
558	+            )
559	+        return span, source_label
560	+    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-SAFE at the outer boundary
561	+        return "", "none"
562	+    finally:
563	+        if owns_client:
564	+            client.close()
565	+

content.content:
2983:def _apply_min_body_stub_gate(
---SIGNATURE---
    extracted_title: str,
    body_type: str,
    jsonld: str,
    doi_hint: str = "",
    pmid_hint: str = "",
) -> tuple[str, bool, str, str, str]:
    """FIX #2 (I-deepfix-001): apply the SAME F14 min-body stub gate that the
    PRIMARY ``_fetch_content`` branch applies (the 3014-3073 block) to the
    naive-httpx FALLBACK return path, which previously returned ``ok=True`` for a
    sub-floor body (an 11-char "Just a moment..." Cloudflare/paywall shell) — a
    dead fetch masquerading as a good source, exactly what F14 was built to stop,
    but only on the fallback path.

    Semantics MIRROR the primary branch: when the body is below the configured
    floor AND a DOI is resolvable, give the OA resolver a chance to UPGRADE the
    shell to full text; otherwise LABEL it ``stub`` (ok=False) — the content is
    STILL returned (a down-weight consumer can inspect it), never DROPPED (§-1.3
    label-not-drop). DEFAULT floor 0 (``PG_FETCH_MIN_BODY_CHARS`` unset) =>
    returns the tuple UNCHANGED => byte-identical to today; the gate fires only on
    the cert sweep (floor=1000). LAW VI: reuses only existing env knobs. The
    faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / span-grounding) is
    downstream and untouched — this is a fetch-layer ok-flag + telemetry change.

    Unlike the primary branch, this helper records ONLY the keyed ``_m45`` reason
    (no ``_trace_tool``): the naive path is reached via ``_fallback_naive_fetch``,
    which already emits the SINGLE per-fetch ``ok``/``fail`` trace for this call —
    mirroring the existing empty-extract precedent (the ``_m45``-only record at
    the ``fetched_200_but_empty_extract`` site below).
    """
    if not ok:
        return content, ok, extracted_title, body_type, jsonld

88	def resolve(key: str) -> str | None:
89	    """Return ``os.getenv(key, CONFIG_DEFAULTS[key])`` — byte-identical to the original call site.
90	
91	    A migrated call site replaces ``os.getenv("PG_X", "<lit>")`` with ``resolve("PG_X")`` where the
92	    registry already holds ``"<lit>"``. Reads the CURRENT environment on every call (like os.getenv).
93	    """

content.text:
Important: `resolve` raises `KeyError` if the key isn't in `CONFIG_DEFAULTS`. `_stub_salvage_enabled` calls `resolve("PG_FETCH_STUB_SALVAGE")` and `.strip()`. If the default is `''` (empty string, not None), `.strip()` works. But note `resolve` can return `None` for keys whose default is `None`. For LEVER B/E/F keys, defaults are `''` (not None), so `.strip()` is safe.

Now the critical check for byte-identity. Let me verify the truthy-check logic and one subtle point: `_stub_salvage_enabled` uses "not in (empty/0/false/...)".
